name: Any @claude issue or comment can invoke a secret-backed agent
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: .github/workflows/claude.yml job condition checked only for '@claude' text in issue/comment/review body — no author_association check — allowing any external actor to trigger the CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN-backed agent by mentioning @claude. Also, 'issues: assigned' trigger fired with the assigner's identity (github.event.sender) rather than the author's author_association, bypassing the association check. Fix implemented in WI-698 (commit a69c6417b, PR #1121): added contains(fromJSON('["OWNER","MEMBER","COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.*.author_association) to every trigger branch; removed id-token: write from permissions; fixed in WI-736 (commit 579091309, PR #1165): removed 'assigned' from issues trigger types. Variants: (a) drive-by external actor mentions @claude in issue comment — now rejected by author_association check; (b) trusted user's issue is assigned by a triage/write user — now blocked by removing 'assigned' trigger; (c) PR review by external actor with @claude — now rejected. Regression verification: run `grep -E 'author_association' .github/workflows/claude.yml` and confirm all four trigger branches include the association check against ["OWNER","MEMBER","COLLABORATOR"]; confirm 'assigned' does not appear in issues.types; confirm workflow-level permissions: {} remains empty. A YAML linter (actionlint or zizmor) must flag a reversion removing the author_association checks. Done: the workflow rejects @claude mentions from actors whose author_association is not OWNER, MEMBER, or COLLABORATOR on every trigger surface.
riskImpact: HIGH severity, high confidence. The job condition only checks whether issue, comment, or review text contains @claude.
bodyText:
Brief
The job condition only checks whether issue, comment, or review text contains @claude. It does not verify author_association, team membership, labels, or maintainer approval before starting anthropics/claude-code-action with CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN and id-token: write. On repos where untrusted users can open issues or comment, an attacker can consume the Claude OAuth quota and drive the agent with prompt content under their control; the unnecessary OIDC permission increases the impact if the action/tooling can run shell commands.
Recommended remediation
Gate invocation to trusted actors, for example OWNER, MEMBER, or COLLABORATOR author associations, or require a maintainer-applied label/approval. Remove id-token: write unless the action truly needs it and configure restrictive Claude tool permissions.
DeepSec finding
Finding ID: DS-006
Scan run: 20260516050543-3902c61be7bd5834 (2026-05-16, deepsec process / gpt-5.5)
File: .github/workflows/claude.yml — lines 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 30, 43, 45
Vulnerability class: missing-auth
Severity: HIGH · Confidence: high
Notes
Captured from the DeepSec scan artefact .deepsec/data/eduagent-build/reports/report.json. Part of bundle WI-76 (WP-ACL). Acceptance Criteria and Execution Path are set during triage/refinement.
